awards: Alfred P. Sloan University Center Fellowship
about:
Originally from Buffalo, NY, Sean Bearden's path to academia was anything but conventional. He began his academic journey while incarcerated, progressing from earning his GED to eventually obtaining a Ph.D. in Physics from UC San Diego. As a member of Dr. Massimiliano Di Ventra's research group, his doctoral work focused on solving complex constraint satisfaction problems using digital memcomputing machines.

Sean is a passionate advocate for nontraditional paths in education and has shared his 'redemption-road' narrative on the Story Collider podcast. He believes that his unique background provides a valuable perspective on perseverance and problem-solving in both scientific research and industry.

Outside of his professional work, Sean is an active practitioner of Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu at P5 Academy in San Diego. He enjoys tinkering with electronics, with past projects including a voice-activated Arduino Deadpool head and various Raspberry Pi applications. Having grown up in Buffalo, he is also a self-proclaimed connoisseur of chicken wings.

interests.name: Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu
interests.description: Active practitioner at P5 Academy in San Diego, finding parallels between the 'gentle art' and complex problem-solving.

interests.name: Creative Engineering
interests.description: Tinkering with Arduino and Raspberry Pi, including a voice-activated Deadpool head project.

interests.name: Buffalo Native
